search: https://www.boyfriendtv.com/search/?q={query}  -- Cloudflare blocks headless requests; not reliable

metadata_mapping.scene_title: JSON-LD VideoObject > name  (also og:title, also <title> minus ' | BoyFriendTV')
metadata_mapping.date: JSON-LD VideoObject > uploadDate (ISO-8601, strip to YYYY-MM-DD)
metadata_mapping.description: JSON-LD VideoObject > description  (comma-separated tag list on user-uploaded content; proper description on studio content)
metadata_mapping.performers: JSON-LD VideoObject > actor[].name  (also href=/pornstars/{slug}/videos/ links in page body)
metadata_mapping.tags: JSON-LD VideoObject > keywords[]  (also href=/tags/{slug}/ anchor text in page body)
metadata_mapping.studio: Constant 'BoyFriendTV' (no per-channel JSON-LD; channel links exist as /channels/{slug}/ but structure varies)

date: Value format: '2025-06-07T15:17:02+00:00' -- split on T and take date part
description: Note: for user-uploaded clips this is a comma-separated list of tags, not a narrative description
performers: Parse JSON-LD VideoObject: json_ld['actor'] list -> each item has 'name' and 'url'
Fallback: CSS selector 'a[href^="/pornstars/"][href$="/videos/"]' to get performer names from page HTML
tags: Fallback: CSS selector 'a[href^="/tags/"]' anchor text in page HTML

search_strategy.notes: https://www.boyfriendtv.com/search/?q={query} exists but Cloudflare returns a JS challenge page for script/headless requests. The scene URL endpoint does NOT trigger Cloudflare when a realistic browser UA and Accept headers are sent. Search and performer pages consistently trigger the challenge. sceneByName and sceneByQueryFragment/sceneByFragment are therefore not reliably implementable without a JS-capable browser (e.g. playwright/selenium), which would be a heavy dependency. Recommend disabling them or documenting as unsupported until a workaround is found.

fragment_matching_strategy.notes: Depends on search, which is blocked by Cloudflare for script requests. Not practical.

anti_bot_or_access_notes: Cloudflare Managed Challenge is active on the site root, search pages (/search/), performer pages (/pornstars/), and the homepage.

Scene detail pages (/videos/{id}/{slug}/) can be fetched successfully with a realistic Firefox/Linux UA, correct Accept and Accept-Language headers, and an 'age_verified=1' cookie. No JS execution needed for scene pages.
A curl fallback (subprocess) is viable for scene pages; Python urllib also works.
Do NOT rely on requests without a CF bypass for search or performer routes.
Age gate cookie required: set 'age_verified=1' in request headers.
known_limitations: sceneByName, sceneByQueryFragment, and sceneByFragment are not supported due to Cloudflare blocking search routes.

performerByURL is blocked by Cloudflare — performer pages trigger JS challenge. The scraper can return partial data (name from URL slug, gender constant) but bio details and photo require a JS-capable fetch.
The JSON-LD 'description' field on user-uploaded clips is tag text, not a narrative synopsis.
No per-scene studio/channel field in JSON-LD; studio is always 'BoyFriendTV'.
CDN URL structure for thumbnails includes a date path segment (e.g. 2025-06/) which makes thumbnail URLs non-reconstructable without fetching the scene page.
